top_signals.title: China Draws Down Billion-Barrel Stockpile as Iran War Cuts Imports in Half
top_signals.summary: The Iran War and the oil and gas market disruption it has caused have prompted a massive drop in Chinese crude oil imports. These were estimated at a little over 6 million barrels daily last month, down from almost 11.40 million barrels daily in February. The twist: China is the country that can afford such a slump, thanks to its inventories. May imports stood at a daily average of 6.36 million barrels, Kpler data cited by Reuters’ Clyde Russell showed this week. This was down from 8.10 million barrels daily for the previous month and from…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Job openings in April surged to 7.6 million, the highest in nearly two years
top_signals.summary: The Bureau of Labor Statistics reported that available employment hit 7.6 million for the month, a surge of 731,000 from the prior month.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Trump signs AI executive order asking companies to give government early access to models
top_signals.summary: AI developers, on a voluntary basis, are asked to collaborate with the government and provide early access to frontier models.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.
